reasoning: The Lean statement captures exactly the informal claim. X is any topological space, A : Set X gets the subspace topology (as a subtype) which is the natural reading of "A ⊂ X". The map r : X → A being Continuous with r x = x for all x : A encodes "continuous retraction r: X → A with r(a) = a for a ∈ A" (equality in the subtype coincides with equality of the underlying elements). Surjectivity onto A follows automatically from the fixed-point condition. IsQuotientMap r is exactly the conclusion "r is a quotient map" (surjective plus codomain topology coinduced by r). No hidden hypotheses or strengthening/weakening is introduced.
